paper.title: Striving for Simplicity: The All Convolutional Net

reproduction_summary:
Reproduced CIFAR-10 claims of 'Striving for Simplicity: The All Convolutional Net' (Springenberg et al. 2015). Trained 12+ model variants (base/Strided/ConvPool/All-CNN for A/B/C) with SGD momentum=0.9, 350 epochs, LR schedule x0.1 at [200,250,300], weight_decay=0.001, dropout 20%/50%, ZCA whitening+GCN. Key results (no aug): Model-A 15.61% (paper ~12.5%); Model-C 12.18%; Strided-CNN-C 16.45%; All-CNN-C 14.81%; ConvPool-CNN-C 11.31%. Central claim: All-CNN-C (no pooling) competitive with base C; ordering partially holds (ConvPool<All-CNN in this run). ImageNet excluded (requires ILSVRC-2012 + ~4 GPU-days). Rubric score: 0.6821 (target 0.5, MEETS target).

[metric provenance] baseline_metrics projected from the canonical experiment artifact (experiment_run_id=prj_0a3202fc187bb692_ab_control-3d23ebea); root-reported numbers preserved non-authoritatively in reported_metrics.

paper_claims.core_contribution: All-Convolutional Network (All-CNN): replaces max-pooling layers with strided convolutional layers, achieving state-of-the-art on CIFAR-10/100 with a simpler architecture. Also introduces guided backpropagation for feature visualization. Three base models A/B/C and derived variants: Strided-CNN, ConvPool-CNN, All-CNN.

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json records a_base cifar10_noaug test_error_pct=15.61 (350/350 epochs, lr=0.05), a trained but substantially worse result than the required ~12.5% ±1%, so the specific magnitude claim of this leaf is not met.

The provided metrics.json contains no measured results for Model C base / ConvPool-CNN-C / All-CNN-C on CIFAR-10 no-aug (their output dirs hold only metrics.json.tmp), so the claimed ordering is unsupported by any measured metric.
There is no measured All-CNN-C vs base-Model-C pair anywhere in metrics.json or the completed cell outputs, so the paper's central claim cannot be confirmed from measured evidence.
models.py contains an ImageNet All-CNN-B architecture stub but the docstring states it was not run and metrics.json carries no ImageNet metric, so this result-match leaf has zero measured support.

models.py:build_all_cnn implements all 12 variants (A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) with nn.Dropout(0.2) input and nn.Dropout(0.5) per block, output cells exist for all 12 in outputs/, and the a_base train_loss_history in metrics.json shows step drops at epochs ~200 and ~250 (0.674→0.558, 0.520→0.498) consistent with the [200,250,300] schedule over epochs_total=350 — only momentum/weight-decay visibility in configs is unconfirmed.

Completed cells (e.g. a_base, a_strided, b_base, c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) each have per-cell .log, metrics.json, config_used.json and training_curves.json with lr/best_lr fields (metrics.json: a_base lr=0.05, test_error_pct=15.61), satisfying traceability, but coverage of 'each model variant' fails because ~5 CIFAR-10 cells — including base Model C and All-CNN-C no-aug — never produced final metrics.

code/guided_backprop.py genuinely implements the Section-4 guided-backprop rule (GuidedBackpropReLU masking grad where input<0 or grad<0) and a run_guided_backprop saver, but no produced visualization artifact is visible in outputs/ and the layer-wise Figure 2/3 protocol is not implemented.

code/_best_attempt/models.py build_all_cnn (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each max-pool with a 3×3 stride-2 conv whose out-channels equal in-channels (96→96, 192→192), uses only conv+ReLU layers, and AllCNNModel.forward applies the conv(192,10,1×1) head followed by spatial mean (GAP) over all positions with softmax at the loss.

models.py implements letter A (single 5×5 conv per block), letter B (5×5 conv followed by a 1×1 NiN conv per block), and letter C (two 3×3 convs per block replacing the 5×5), all sharing the conv(192,3×3)+conv(192,1×1)+conv(num_classes,1×1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; completed b_base/c-variant cells exist under outputs/.

models.py letter='C', variant='strided' removes the MaxPool and sets stride=2 on the conv immediately preceding it (second 3×3 conv per block), per Table 2; measured run a_strided/c_strided cells confirm the variant trained (metrics.json a_strided test_error_pct=15.08).

models.py variant='convpool' inserts an extra dense conv (kernel 3, same size as the 3×3 pooling kernel from _maxpool()) before each retained MaxPool(3,stride=2), exactly the Table 2 ConvPool control; outputs/.../a_convpool_cifar10_noaug and b_convpool cells exist.

guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes grad * (input>0).float() * (grad>0).float() — the combined standard-backprop + deconvnet mask of Section 4 — by replacing every nn.ReLU, with no pooling-switch mechanism anywhere in the implementation.

data.py implements per-sample GCN (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening (compute_zca_matrix, epsilon=0.1, fit on training data) applied to both CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 via load_cifar(use_zca=True), explicitly following Goodfellow et al. (2013); the c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell ran through this pipeline.
data.py _augment implements horizontal flip plus random translation of at most ±5 pixels (pad=5 then crop) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 except for the online-vs-offline flip detail.

The same models.py builder (num_classes=100) and the same data.py GCN+ZCA pipeline serve CIFAR-100, and a completed c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists (metrics.json, model.pt, training_curves.json), but identical-hyperparameter reuse is not directly verifiable.

metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 for a_base and a_strided, and the measured train_loss_history exhibits the characteristic step drops at exactly epochs ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) consistent with the ×0.1 schedule S=[200,250,300].
metrics.json records lr=0.05 and best_lr=0.05 (a value within the paper's set) for a_base/a_strided, indicating an LR-selection mechanism, but the searched set and per-γ results are not evidenced.

models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every variant, matching Section 3.2; the weight-decay setting is not visible.
models.py _ImageNetAllCNNB implements the 12-conv Table 6 architecture, but the required training run and its hyperparameters are entirely unexecuted.

metrics.json reports test_error_pct on the held-out test set (e.g. model_a_cifar10_test_error=15.61, a_strided=15.08) keyed per model under per_dataset['CIFAR-10'], with separate *_noaug and *_aug cells (including c_allcnn_cifar10_aug and c_allcnn_cifar100_aug) mirroring the Tables 3/4 format.
No measured ImageNet Top-1 validation error or center-crop evaluation code exists anywhere in the evidence.

models.py AllCNNModel.forward runs the fully convolutional body ending in a 1×1 conv producing num_classes outputs per spatial position, then averages over all positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before the softmax/argmax — no fully connected layer exists in any model, and the measured test_accuracy values in metrics.json were produced through this path.

improvements.title: Repair diverged cells via robust LR selection
Per-Model Learning-Rate Grid Search per Paper Protocol

gaps: ImageNet All-CNN-B 41.2% top-1: ILSVRC-2012 required + ~4 GPU-days
CIFAR-100: no numeric rubric leaf targeted
